20/20 pass dev+non-dev, B stable over 3 repeats. mcast all-gather via Counter staging (avoids Flag self-set stale-flag on rotating sender), src==dst EXCLUDE_SRC trick + local-copy own slot.
Full grid-saturation heuristic: B when Ht_total<cores and rectangular K adds cores. 20/20 dev+non-dev x2. Routes K=8/16/32/64, layouts 8x1..8x8 + 2-band.

Two compounding bugs at cb_partial_sumsq cross-thread handshake. BUG1 (1/num_chunks): reader cb_wait_front(cb_partial_sumsq,1) returns after PASS-1 chunk0 push (count=1) and grabs that front L1 addr; reduce-accumulate pops/repushes across chunks landing the final sum in a DIFFERENT physical page, so reader mcasts only chunk0 partial = local/num_chunks. BUG2 (1/2): combine copy<gathered,partial_sumsq>+in-place add reads STALE PASS-1 local_sumsq as front because reader pops cb_partial_sumsq only after pushing gathered. Fix: dedicated cb_local_sumsq single-push handoff (compute->reader) after PASS-1 completes; combine writes into emptied cb_partial_sumsq.

Added cb_local_sumsq (CB28) single-push handoff. Compute Regime B: after PASS-1, copy<cb_partial_sumsq,cb_local_sumsq> (pops accumulator, pushes once after full accumulation), reader reads cb_local_sumsq, combine writes emptied cb_partial_sumsq. Wired CT args in reader_mcast + compute + descriptor (both regimes).
Regime B 8 shapes (nc=1,2,3; gamma off): all-ones mean=1.0000; random PCC=0.99999 relRMS 0.0035-0.0091. Bug fixed.
39 new Regime B tests pass (all-ones exact, distinguishable shards, random vs torch +/-gamma, LOOSE 16384/32768). Existing 20/20 still pass.

Bounded cb_normalized: chunked gamma pass-2 by REDUCE_BLOCK (Col->Row streaming), cb_normalized sized reduce_block (was Wt/Wt_s). recip held across chunks + manual pop per row; gamma held resident indexed by per-chunk TileOffset; input streams in order. Per-core L1 no longer scales with Wt.
Unit 67 passed; golden 43 passed 0 failed 0 xpass after chunking. --dev probe (multi-row gamma reuse, num_chunks up to 4) clean, no hangs/asserts.
Refinement 1 [x]: Regime B correctness fixed (golden 22->43, 0 fail, 0 drift). cb_normalized bounded to REDUCE_BLOCK. No SUPPORTED axis added (Regime B is shape/L1-selected). 39 new tests.

bf8b input + Regime B mcast all-gather produces Inf in a subset of output elements. bf8b Regime A passes; bf16/fp32 Regime B passes. Suspect bf8b shard read/square or cross-core fp32 partial path interaction. Reproducing to localize.

mcast reader: use get_tile_size(cb_partials_gathered) (partial_tile_bytes) for all-gather slot stride + local copy + sender.send, not input tile_bytes. Latent assumption input-tile==partial-tile broke when bf8b input (1088B) != fp32 partials (4096B).

note: RM probe: no-gamma + RM-gamma + fp32 all OK (pcc~1.0). TILE-layout gamma BAD (pcc 0.17-0.55). Root cause: RM reader reads gamma as RM sticks but TILE gamma is physically tiled. Need gamma_is_tile branch: read gamma tiles directly into cb_gamma_tiled, skip tilize.

A-vs-B crossover sweep (1,1,32,W) bf16 no_gamma: A scales linearly with Wt (27.7us@Wt32 -> 196us@Wt256, single core); B ~flat 71-110us (K-way parallel, fixed mcast cost). A wins Wt<=64 (1.5-2x), B wins Wt>=192 (1.4-1.8x), wash 96-128. Crossover ~Wt160.

Regime A rows/core scaling (W=256,Wt=8): 1->17.8us, 2->31us, 4->51us, 8->92.7us, 16->181us. Marginal per-row ~10-11us (flat). Fixed overhead ~7us. Per-row cost is data-work-bound not init-bound -> row-blocking ceiling is modest (init amortization only, est ~10%).

Crossover sends (1,1,32,4096) fp32+gamma (Wt=128<160) to Regime A. row_fits uses RESIDENT_BUDGET_TILES=560 tile-count, dtype-agnostic, and omits R5-enlarged cb_squared(Wt). Real Regime A footprint=input+squared+gamma=3*Wt*tile_bytes; fp32 Wt=128 = 1.57MB resident -> OOM 1.75MB total. Fix: byte-aware row_fits counting input+squared+gamma in actual formats.
3 passed: baseline table + 2 crossover. OOM fixed via byte-aware row_fits.

RM A-vs-B crossover (1,1,32,W): A wins Wt=64 (75 vs 107us), B wins Wt>=128 (151 vs 108us @128). RM crossover ~96, LOWER than TILE ~160 because RM Regime A tilizes whole row on one core. Need layout-aware threshold: TILE=160, RM=96. Wt=384 A=121us is byte-budget fallback-to-B (confirms OOM floor).
5 passed: baseline + TILE(Wt64->A,Wt256->B) + RM(Wt64->A,Wt128->B)

test_translated test_rms_norm_row_major is actually TILE input (seed=0, deterministic). 12 failures = TILE (1,128,4096) Wt=128 +gamma, all fp32_dest_acc_en=False. My crossover (Wt=128<160) routes them B->A; Regime A wide reduce(128 tiles) in bf16 dest (fp32_acc=False) loses precision -> Frobenius 0.052-0.056 > 0.052. B (narrow shards) preserved it. Fix: fp32_acc-aware crossover: fp32_acc=False -> lower threshold (96) so wide rows stay in B; fp32_acc=True -> A reduce is fp32-accumulated, precise, keep perf crossover 160.
fp32_acc-aware crossover: _b_min_wt(layout_is_rm, fp32_acc). When fp32_acc=False, threshold=min(perf_crossover, 96) so wide bf16 rows stay in Regime B (precise). test_translated 99/99 restored.
R5(force-B) vs R6(heuristic): TILE Wt32=1.53x, Wt64=2.0x, Wt128=1.05x, Wt256=1.0x; RM Wt32=1.14x, Wt64=1.42x, Wt128/256=1.0x. No regression. golden 1683/1683, translated 99/99.

K-sweep (force K, TILE): (1,1,32,8192)Wt256: K8=35.7us K16=33.9us K32=50.5us K64=109.8us. (1,1,32,16384)Wt512: K8=60us K16=48us K32=59us K64=116us. Maximize-K (K=64) is WORST (3x slower than K=16). All-gather cost ~O(K) dominates. Optimal K minimizes proxy Wt/K + K (~16). Retune _select_k from max-K to proxy-min-K; needs byte-aware shard budget.

Re-measured crossover with K-tuned B: B now wins from Wt>=16 both layouts (TILE Wt8 A=9.8 B=11.3->A; Wt16 A=15.7 B=12->B; Wt128 A=99.9 B=22.9->B 4x). RM same (Wt8->A, Wt16+->B). K-tuning made B ~6x faster so crossover dropped from ~160 to ~12-16. New threshold=16 both layouts. Precision ceiling(96) now inert (perf crossover 16<96).

K-tuning: _select_k now minimizes proxy (Wt//K + K) + byte-aware shard budget, instead of maximizing K. (1,1,32,8192) bf16 TILE: 109.9->34.0us (3.2x). Crossover retuned to 16 (B wins from Wt>=16). golden 1782, precision 649, aux 251, perf 5 all pass.
Wide-W Regime B 2.1-6.1x faster (1,1,32,2048 TILE 103.8->17.0us=6.1x). Many-row Regime A unchanged. Changelog + Refinement 7 (row-blocking follow-up) filed. Checkbox -> [~].
